appliedUpdates.description: Ruff auto-fix removed unused imports in models.py and model_management.py (typing.Optional, get_best_model_name, os, datetime, hashlib). No services.yaml changes needed.
Added logging import and logger instance to fix NameError at runtime
Fixed error event format to use {'type': 'error', 'error': ...} so API client recognizes them

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the logging convention: when creating new router/service files, include `import logging` and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` as a standard pattern. Currently only settings.py and session.py show this pattern, but it's not codified.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: llm-model-management failed because worker didn't add logger import. llm-ram-detection and llm-marcus-persona also followed this pattern correctly (from reading other files), suggesting it's an established convention but undocumented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit step in Work Procedure for logging setup: 'Add logging import and logger instance to new router/service files'. Also add note about SSE event format conventions for streaming endpoints.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: llm-model-management: worker missed logger import. llm-streaming-responses: worker used inconsistent error event format (`{'error'}` vs `{'type': 'error'}`). Both are convention gaps in the skill.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SSE event format standard: events should include `type` field (e.g., `{'type': 'token', 'content': '...'}`, `{'type': 'error', 'error': '...'}`). Current session.py has mixed formats - 'token', 'session_state', 'complete' use type field but errors don't.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: llm-streaming-responses review identified that session.py:302 and :318 yield `{'error': '...'}` without type field, while the same file uses `{'type': 'token'}` for tokens. This inconsistency caused the error handling bug.
